title: ICUBE-Q Lunar Mission
shortDescription: ICUBE-Q is Pakistan's lunar CubeSat mission and flew aboard China's Chang'e-6 mission. The lunar opportunity was opened to APSCO member states; Pakistan's proposal and ICUBE-Q team were selected, and Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as one of three Principal Investigators. A Chang'e-6 payload, ICUBE-Q gave Pakistan visible lunar-mission credibility.
description: ICUBE-Q is Pakistan's lunar CubeSat mission and flew aboard China's Chang'e-6 mission. The lunar orbital opportunity was opened to APSCO member states, Pakistan's proposal and ICUBE-Q team were selected, and Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as one of three Principal Investigators. As one of the payloads associated with the Chang'e-6 mission, ICUBE-Q contributed to Pakistan's visible lunar-mission capability and public space-technology credibility.
partners: China National Space Administration, Chang'e-6 mission

sections.title: Mission Overview
sections.content: ICUBE-Q is Pakistan's lunar CubeSat mission and flew aboard China's Chang'e-6 mission. The lunar orbital opportunity was opened to APSCO member states, Pakistan's proposal and ICUBE-Q team were selected, and Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as one of three Principal Investigators. As one of the payloads associated with the Chang'e-6 mission, ICUBE-Q contributed to Pakistan's visible lunar-mission capability and public space-technology credibility.

title: SSS-2A
shortDescription: SSS-2A is part of APSCO's Student Small Satellite (SSS) program, which includes SSS-1, SSS-2A, and SSS-2B. From the Pakistan side, Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as the technical lead / expert and worked on ADCS, power, and communications in collaboration with Shanghai Jiao Tong University.
description: SSS-2A is part of APSCO's Student Small Satellite (SSS) program, which includes SSS-1, SSS-2A, and SSS-2B. From the Pakistan side, Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as the technical lead / expert and worked on ADCS, power, and communications in collaboration with Shanghai Jiao Tong University. The program reflects structured international small-satellite collaboration and student-oriented capability development.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: SSS-2A is part of APSCO's Student Small Satellite (SSS) program, which includes SSS-1, SSS-2A, and SSS-2B. From the Pakistan side, Dr. Rehan Mahmood served as the technical lead / expert and worked on ADCS, power, and communications in collaboration with Shanghai Jiao Tong University. The program reflects structured international small-satellite collaboration and student-oriented capability development.

title: ICUBE-N
shortDescription: ICUBE-N reflects national CubeSat capability-building with emphasis on indigenous architecture, subsystem development, and mission-oriented nanosatellite engineering through SSTRL and associated programs.
description: ICUBE-N reflects national CubeSat capability-building with current emphasis on indigenous architecture, subsystem development, and mission-oriented nanosatellite engineering through SSTRL and associated programs.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: ICUBE-N reflects national CubeSat capability-building with current emphasis on indigenous architecture, subsystem development, and mission-oriented nanosatellite engineering through SSTRL and associated programs.

title: ICUBE-1
shortDescription: ICUBE-1 was Pakistan's first CubeSat and launched on 21 November 2013. Dr. Rehan Mahmood served in a project-management and senior systems-engineering role, contributing across subsystem/software work, environmental testing, launch-campaign participation, and broader mission execution support.
description: ICUBE-1 was Pakistan's first CubeSat and launched on 21 November 2013. Dr. Rehan Mahmood served in a project-management and senior systems-engineering role, contributing across subsystem/software work, environmental testing, launch-campaign participation, and broader mission execution support. The mission represents a foundational step in Pakistan's CubeSat capability development.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: ICUBE-1 was Pakistan's first CubeSat and launched on 21 November 2013. Dr. Rehan Mahmood served in a project-management and senior systems-engineering role, contributing across subsystem/software work, environmental testing, launch-campaign participation, and broader mission execution support. The mission represents a foundational step in Pakistan's CubeSat capability development.

title: NGN/NTN–PPDR Simulator
shortDescription: Active simulator and digital-twin direction for resilient communications across terrestrial and non-terrestrial network environments, especially for public protection and disaster relief (PPDR). The public website shows a representative frontend preview; the broader direction includes scenario evaluation, KPI-driven analysis, telecom-space convergence, and future operator-facing and policy-facing interfaces.
description: The NGN/NTN–PPDR simulator reflects an active research and development direction around scenario evaluation, digital-twin thinking, resilient communications, and future operator-facing / policy-facing interfaces for terrestrial and non-terrestrial network environments. The public-facing site presents a representative frontend preview built from the broader simulator direction.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The NGN/NTN–PPDR simulator reflects an active research and development direction around scenario evaluation, digital-twin thinking, resilient communications, and future operator-facing / policy-facing interfaces for terrestrial and non-terrestrial network environments. The public-facing site presents a representative frontend preview built from the broader simulator direction.

title: KidSat
shortDescription: Directed classroom satellite and STEM initiatives designed to bring mission-based space engineering into education.
description: Directed classroom satellite and STEM initiatives designed to bring mission-based space engineering into education.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: KidSat and related classroom satellite initiatives bring mission-based space engineering into education, engaging young students in satellite technology and space science through hands-on activities.

title: ICUBE-CSAT
shortDescription: Directed classroom satellite and STEM initiatives in the ICUBE program series.
description: CubeSat in the ICUBE program series, contributing to mission-based space engineering and STEM capability development.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: ICUBE-CSAT contributes to the broader ICUBE program objectives and mission-based space engineering education.
